title: taraxacum grypodiforme
intro: Taraxacum grypodiforme, also known as the Horse's Ear or False Dandelion, is a flowering plant species in the sunflower family native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's leaves, roots, and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Taraxacum grypodiforme include relief from urinary tract issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
